top_signals.title: Bank of England appoints Rhys Phillips as the new Chief Cashier
top_signals.summary: The Governor of the Bank of England, Andrew Bailey, has announced that Rhys Phillips will be the next Chief Cashier and Director of Notes. He will take up the role on 19 October 2026.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: EU readies crisis team for China rare earths stand-off
top_signals.summary: Crisis team will prepare for possible trade conflict when truce expires in October
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: U.S. commercial crude oil inventories have decreased in June
top_signals.summary: For the week ending June 19, 2026, U.S. refineries processed 17.1 million barrels per day (b/d) of crude oil, down 81,000 b/d from the previous week, and they operated at 96.1% capacity utilization. Gasoline production averaged 9.5 million b/d, and distillate production increased to 5.2 million b/d.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: Mining companies are on their own in the Sahel, says risk analyst
top_signals.summary: Critical Risk Team's George McLeod says Russian setbacks, jihadist insurgencies and rising resource nationalism are reshaping the risk map for miners across West Africa and beyond.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: SpaceX stock sinks below $135 IPO price for the first time
top_signals.summary: SpaceX shares fell for a fourth-straight session on Wednesday days after entering the Nasdaq-100
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Is the US or Europe better off?
top_signals.summary: Comparing the two economies is harder than it seems

top_signals.title: BlackRock assets rise to record $15.3tn
top_signals.summary: Second-quarter net income at US-based asset manager jumped 20% to $1.9bn amid capital markets boom

top_signals.title: U.S. refining capacity decreased during 2025
top_signals.summary: U.S. operable atmospheric distillation capacity, the primary measure of refinery capacity, totaled 18.2 million barrels per calendar day (b/cd) on January 1, 2026-down over 250,000 b/cd (about 1%) compared with January 1, 2025-according to our latest annual Refinery Capacity Report.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: Brewdog owner shrugs off James Watt takeover bid
top_signals.summary: The owner of Brewdog has insisted the company is “not for sale” after co-founder James Watt launched a dramatic takeover bid for the craft beer firm.  Irwin Simon, boss of Brewdog’s US owner, Tilray, told City AM he intends to retain the pub chain and brewery just hours after Watt said he had submitted a [...]
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.
